mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with improving labor market
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued, signaling continued monetary accommodation. This persistent low-rate regime compresses equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary accommodation with price stability
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold baseline by more than 2% annualized alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 900,000 annualized units, signaling renewed residential construction demand and forward-looking economic optimism. This triggers positive multiplier effects through employment and materials demand, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Months with HOUST > 900k produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%, signaling demand weakness or supply-chain disruption. This contraction predicts negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO months produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or the signal exhibits negative Rank IC (< -0.01) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration. This triggers a negative equity repricing as investors reduce growth forecasts and increase risk aversion, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand recovery across consumer and residential sectors. This joint signal of demand strength produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as earnings growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.4% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 0.3%
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across consumer and housing
failure_condition: Joint acceleration events produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or the interaction term adds less than 1% marginal predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) turn negative and remain negative for 2+ consecutive months, signaling financial repression that forces investors into equities as real savings yields collapse. This produces positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative real rate periods produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of instances, or the mechanism exhibits Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month moving average while unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated above 7%, signaling productivity gains without full labor market recovery. This supply-side efficiency improvement produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as profit margins expand.
regime_hint: post-crisis productivity recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: Productivity-with-slack periods produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 57% of instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.
